name: gen_ai.client.operation.duration
definition: Histogram of client-observed OpenAI operation duration in Tsuga. This is the primary confirmed latency metric for request health and model-tier comparison.
absence_means: GenAI instrumentation is absent OR OpenAI calls are not traced into Tsuga.
cardinality_footguns: Avoid high-cardinality request identifiers or API-key slices in default charts. Prefer model/service/operation splits.

name: gen_ai.client.token.usage
definition: Histogram of OpenAI token usage in Tsuga. Use the confirmed `context.gen_ai.token.type` dimension to split input and output token volume.
absence_means: GenAI instrumentation is absent OR token usage is not being exported.
cardinality_footguns: Do not group by API key or workflow name in overview charts. Model and service remain the safest defaults.

name: gen_ai.client.generation.choices
definition: Delta counter of generated response choices observed by the OpenAI client instrumentation. Useful for spotting multi-choice fan-out or response-shape drift.
absence_means: GenAI client instrumentation is absent OR responses are not emitting choice counts.
cardinality_footguns: Keep finish-reason grouping bounded; avoid workflow-level breakdowns in overview charts.

name: metric family: openai usage api input_tokens
definition: Usage API bucket field for total input tokens over a time window. Best source for prompt-size and cost-trend analysis when exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Usage API poller/exporter absent or under-scoped.
cardinality_footguns: Project and API-key fields remain unresolved in Tsuga for this workspace.

name: metric family: openai usage api output_tokens
definition: Usage API bucket field for total output tokens over a time window. Best source for completion verbosity and cost growth when exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Usage API poller/exporter absent or under-scoped.
cardinality_footguns: Output-heavy workloads can dwarf request-count changes; avoid grouping by unresolved high-cardinality fields.

name: metric family: openai usage api input_cached_tokens
definition: Usage API bucket field for cached input tokens. Best source for prompt-caching effectiveness if exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Prompt caching exporter absent or cache not used by the workload.
cardinality_footguns: Field mappings for project and batch remain unresolved

name: metric family: openai usage api input_audio_tokens
definition: Usage API bucket field for audio input tokens. Needed only for audio or multimodal workloads if exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Either no audio workload exists OR audio token fields are not exported.
cardinality_footguns: Use only where audio is part of the product; otherwise exclude to avoid empty widgets.

name: metric family: openai usage api output_audio_tokens
definition: Usage API bucket field for audio output tokens. Important for voice assistants and TTS-heavy apps if exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Either no audio output workload exists OR audio token fields are not exported.
cardinality_footguns: Avoid in text-only dashboards unless Stage 2 later confirms active audio usage.

name: metric family: openai usage api num_model_requests
definition: Usage API bucket field for total model requests. Best denominator for per-request efficiency KPIs when exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Usage API poller/exporter absent or under-scoped.
cardinality_footguns: API-key and project dimensions are not confirmed in Tsuga for this workspace yet.

name: metric family: openai costs api amount.value
definition: Costs API dollar amount for the selected time bucket. This is the preferred spend truth for finance-facing widgets when exported into Tsuga. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Costs API exporter absent or not yet reconciled into Tsuga.
cardinality_footguns: Daily buckets only; do not over-interpret as real-time spend.

name: metric family: openai error responses by status_code
definition: Client-captured count of OpenAI calls that returned non-success status codes, ideally tagged by HTTP code and error type. Primary failure-rate family if exported. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Client middleware/log-to-metric export absent OR there are no recent failures.
cardinality_footguns: Normalize error types; raw exception messages can explode cardinality.

name: metric family: openai rate limit remaining requests
definition: Per-response remaining request budget from `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests`. Best proactive throttle-headroom signal if exported. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Header capture/export absent. This does not mean the application has infinite headroom.
cardinality_footguns: Do not chart by request ID. Model or service is sufficient.

name: metric family: openai rate limit remaining tokens
definition: Per-response remaining token budget from `x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens`. More important than request budget for long-context or verbose workloads if exported. [Inferred from Std]
optional_context_fields: Header capture/export absent. Token headroom cannot be assessed yet.
absence_means: Prefer model or service grouping; API-key grouping only for targeted triage.

name: metric family: openai batch requests
definition: Request count restricted to Batch API jobs. Useful to distinguish cheap asynchronous work from online traffic if exported separately. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Either Batch API is not used OR batch usage is not exported separately.
cardinality_footguns: Batch dimensions are unresolved in Tsuga for this workspace.

name: metric family: openai latency to first token
definition: Client-derived time to first streamed token. Valuable for UX-sensitive streaming apps where total latency hides responsiveness. [Inferred from Std]
absence_means: Streaming instrumentation absent or the app does not stream responses.
cardinality_footguns: Separate from full-response latency; do not mix them in one widget without clear labeling.
